input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Both .NET 9 and 10 runtimes are available. Let me create a BenchmarkDotNet project to compare performance across both frameworks.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-9668605646fe40b8989af574250858b4 && mkdir -p TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks && cat > TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj << 'EOF'
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.0" />
    <ProjectReference Include="..\TextAnalyzer\TextAnalyzer.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
EOF

data.content: Need to fix the CoreRuntime reference for .NET 10. Let me use the proper API:
data.reasoningText:
The BenchmarkDotNet version 0.14.0 might not have `CoreRuntime.Core100` yet. Let me check what's available or use a different approach. I could use `CoreRuntime.CreateForNewVersion("net10.0", ".NET 10.0")` or use the attribute-based job configuration with `[SimpleJob(RuntimeMoniker.Net90)]` etc. Let me check what's available.

Actually, let me try using `RuntimeMoniker` or creating a custom runtime. Alternatively, I can try BenchmarkDotNet 0.15.0 or a newer version that might support .NET 10.


I'll try the `[SimpleJob]` attribute with `RuntimeMoniker` or manually construct a `CoreRuntime` to work around the version limitations.

data.content: BDN 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10 multi-targeting. Let me simplify - run each framework separately with default job:
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't support .NET 10 well. Let me try a newer version, or use a different approach. Let me check if there's a newer BDN version available that supports .NET 10, or I can use the `--runtimes` CLI option.

Actually, maybe I should try BenchmarkDotNet 0.15.x or a nightly build. Or I could use `CreateForNewVersion` differently. Let me try a different approach - use BDN's `--runtimes` CLI flag which might work better.

Actually the problem is that BDN 0.14.0 doesn't know about .NET 10. 

I could upgrade to a newer BenchmarkDotNet version that recognizes .NET 10, or I could sidestep the multi-runtime orchestration entirely by running the benchmark separately for each framework and comparing the results manually.
